key_points: A figure has line symmetry if it can be folded into two identical halves
The fold line is called the 'Line of Symmetry' or 'Axis of Symmetry'
Regular polygons have multiple lines of symmetry
Mirror reflection is a form of line symmetry where the object and image are equidistant from the line
examples: One line of symmetry: Isosceles triangle, Letter 'A', Butterfly
Two lines of symmetry: Rectangle, Letter 'H'
Three lines of symmetry: Equilateral triangle
formulas: Regular polygon symmetry lines $\rightarrow$ Number of sides ($n$)
Distance to mirror $\rightarrow$ Object distance = Image distance
exam_tips: Use a mirror to check for reflection symmetry in complex shapes
Vertical and horizontal lines of symmetry are different; check for both
In regular polygons, lines of symmetry pass through vertices or midpoints of sides
Remember: A scalene triangle has zero lines of symmetry
